message: fix(installer): robust TTY prompts + bootstrap libs

files.patch:
@@ -155,6 +155,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-axx","title":"Fix update flow + correct workspace fix hints","description":"- scripts/lib/update.sh currently exits on first failed update because run_cmd returns non-zero under set -e; should continue and summarize failures. - scripts/lib/doctor.sh + scripts/lib/smoke_test.sh suggest chowning /data (not /data/projects) which doesn't fix writability.","acceptance_criteria":"- acfs update continues running all update steps even if one fails; final exit code reflects FAIL_COUNT. - doctor/smoke_test workspace fix hints correctly chown /data/projects (and handle missing /etc/os-release without noisy errors).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:16:52.910887-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:25:29.646034-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:25:29.646034-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed update.sh counter/return semantics; corrected doctor/smoke workspace fix hints; improved doctor OS handling + JSON escaping."} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-azw","title":"Implement database and cloud CLI functional tests","description":"# Task: Implement database and cloud CLI functional tests ## Context Part of EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i) ## What to Do Create functional tests for databases and cloud CLIs: ### PostgreSQL Tests - check_postgres_connection(): psql -c "SELECT 1" - check_postgres_role(): Verify ubuntu role exists ### Cloud CLI Tests - check_vault_configured(): VAULT_ADDR set (info status if not) - check_wrangler_auth(): wrangler whoami - check_supabase_auth(): Access token exists and not expired - check_vercel_auth(): vercel whoami ### Status Codes - 0: Pass - 1: Fail (with fix suggestion) - 2: Skip (not installed) - 3: Info (not configured, but optional) ## Acceptance Criteria - Each cloud CLI has auth check - PostgreSQL connection and role verified - Status codes used consistently - Timeout on external calls (prevent hangs) ## Files to Modify - scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add cloud/db check functions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:20.261596-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:59:50.118089-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:59:50.118089-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented check_postgres_connection(), check_postgres_role(), and enhanced cloud CLI checks","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-azw","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-01s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:35.134842-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b1yk","title":"FEATURE: Installer UX Improvements","description":"## Parent Epic agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-umqa (Beginner UX Overhaul v1.0) ## Summary Improve the user experience during the 10-15 minute installation process. Currently, users see scrolling text with no clear indication of progress or expected duration. This causes anxiety and premature terminal closing. ## Background & Motivation The UX audit noted: > "15 minutes of waiting with scrolling text is scary... User thought: > 'Is it stuck? Should I do something? When will this end?'" Users who close their terminal during installation cause failed setups and support burden. Clear progress indicators reduce anxiety and improve completion rates. ## Components ### 1. Progress Header (Always Visible) Add a persistent header that shows: \`\`\` ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ Agent Flywheel Installer v0.1.0 ║ ║ Progress: [████████████░░░░░░░░] 60% (8 of 13 modules) ║ ║ Current: Installing programming languages... ║ ║ Elapsed: 5m 23s | Remaining: ~3-4 min ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ \`\`\` This should update in-place (using \\r or terminal control codes). ### 2. Module-Level Progress Each module shows: - [1/13] Setting up user account... - [2/13] Installing shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh)... - [3/13] Installing programming languages... - etc. ### 3. Time Estimates Based on benchmarks: - User setup: ~30 seconds - Shell: ~60 seconds - Languages (bun, uv, rust, go): ~180 seconds - Dev tools: ~120 seconds - Cloud CLIs: ~90 seconds - Agents: ~60 seconds - Stack tools: ~120 seconds - Database: ~180 seconds (if enabled) - Total: ~12-15 minutes Show "Remaining: ~X min" based on current progress. ### 4. Clear Completion Signal When done: \`\`\` ╔════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗ ║ ✓ Installation Complete! ║ ╠════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣ ║ ║ ║ Total time: 12m 34s ║ ║ Modules installed: 13/13 ║ ║ ║ ║ NEXT STEPS: ║ ║ 1. Type 'exit' to disconnect ║ ║ 2. Reconnect with: ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_IP ║ ║ 3. Start coding with 'cc' (Claude Code) ║ ║ ║ ╚════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝ \`\`\` ### 5. Troubleshooting Guidance If a step fails: \`\`\` ⚠ Warning: Module 'install_rust' had issues Common causes: • Network timeout - will retry automatically • Package mirror busy - usually resolves on retry The installer will continue with other modules. You can run the installer again to retry failed modules. \`\`\` ## Technical Implementation ### Progress Tracking The installer already has phases/modules. We need to: 1. Count total modules at start 2. Track current module number 3. Calculate elapsed time 4. Estimate remaining based on benchmarks \`\`\`bash # Global tracking TOTAL_MODULES=13 CURRENT_MODULE=0 START_TIME=$(date +%s) # Progress function show_progress() { local current=$1 local name=$2 local percent=$((current * 100 / TOTAL_MODULES)) local elapsed=$(($(date +%s) - START_TIME)) local remaining=$((elapsed * (TOTAL_MODULES - current) / current)) 2>/dev/null || 0 # Clear line and print progress printf "\\r[%d/%d] %3d%% | Elapsed: %dm %ds | Remaining: ~%dm | %s" \\ "$current" "$TOTAL_MODULES" "$percent" \\ $((elapsed/60)) $((elapsed%60)) \\ $((remaining/60)) "$name" } \`\`\` ### Terminal Compatibility - Use ANSI escape codes for in-place updates - Fall back to simple output if terminal doesn't support - Handle both interactive and non-interactive modes ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Progress percentage visible throughout installation - [ ] Current module name displayed - [ ] Elapsed time shown - [ ] Estimated remaining time shown - [ ] Clear completion message with next steps - [ ] Failed module guidance (don't panic) - [ ] Works in both SSH terminal and console - [ ] No breaking changes to existing installer logic ## Dependencies - FEATURE: Installer SSH Key Integration - Should integrate progress for key setup step too ## Testing Plan 1. Run full installation and verify progress updates 2. Test with slow network (progress should still work) 3. Test with failed module (guidance should appear) 4. Test in different terminals (iTerm, Terminal.app, Windows Terminal) 5. Test via tmux (should still work) ## Future Enhancements - Web-based progress dashboard (installer pings API) - Sound notification on completion - Desktop notification via terminal hooks","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:17:52.137253-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:34:51.998446-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:34:51.998446-05:00","close_reason":"Design specification complete. Detailed progress display requirements, terminal compatibility notes, and time estimation logic documented. Closing to unblock implementation task 3u3m.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b1yk","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fslp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:18:20.725081-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b6m9","title":"Installer: fix interactivity + avoid mixed refs by using bootstrap libs","description":"Deep-dive found installer interactivity + mixed-ref risks: - install.sh exports ACFS_INTERACTIVE=false by default, forcing non-interactive checksum mismatch handling. - security.sh/_acfs_is_interactive and state.sh confirm_resume gate on -t 0, so curl|bash + /dev/tty cannot prompt even when safe. - run_preflight_checks and ubuntu_upgrade loader re-download scripts from ACFS_RAW even after bootstrap archive is present, reintroducing mixed refs. - install.sh loads both context.sh and error_tracking.sh (try_step vs try_step_eval split). Fix: make TTY detection rely on /dev/tty (open test), keep resume prompts opt-in, and source preflight/ubuntu_upgrade/report from ACFS_BOOTSTRAP_DIR/ACFS_LIB_DIR. Add try_step_eval to error_tracking.sh and stop early network-sourcing of libs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-28T20:18:16.36819-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-28T20:44:13.586584-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T20:44:13.586584-05:00","close_reason":"Fix curl|bash interactivity + mixed-ref bootstrap; source reporting; fix resume gum prompt"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b9v","title":"Task: Update Step 12 with Learning Hub integration","description":"# Update Step 12 with Learning Hub Integration ## Parent Feature Wizard Integration (mna) ## Goal Update the wizard's final step (launch-onboarding/page.tsx) to prominently feature the new Learning Hub as the primary continuation path. ## Current State Step 12 currently: 1. Shows celebration confetti 2. Has "Start the onboarding tutorial" card with `onboard` command 3. Shows "What you can do now" quick reference 4. Has "Continue to Part Two: The Workflow" link ## Proposed Changes ### Update the Main CTA Replace the command-focused card with a Learning Hub card: Before: ```tsx

Start the onboarding tutorial

Learn the basics with an interactive tutorial:

 ``` After: ```tsx

Continue Your Learning Journey

Master your new environment with 9 guided lessons:

{/* Primary CTA */} {/* Secondary option */}
Prefer the terminal? Run onboard
``` ### Update "Part Two" Messaging The card that links to `/workflow` should mention the Learning Hub: ```tsx

Ready for the Advanced Workflow?

After completing the Learning Hub basics, dive into the powerful multi-agent workflow that lets you build production-ready software at incredible speed.

``` ### Visual Hierarchy 1. **Celebration header** - "Congratulations!" (keep as-is) 2. **Learning Hub CTA** - Primary call-to-action (NEW) 3. **Quick reference cards** - "What you can do now" (keep as-is) 4. **Part Two card** - Advanced workflow (update messaging) ### Analytics Update Track clicks to Learning Hub: ```typescript const { trackEvent } = useWizardAnalytics(); // In the Learning Hub button onClick trackEvent('learning_hub_started', { from: 'step_12' }); ``` ## File Changes - Update: `apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Learning Hub is the primary CTA - [ ] CLI option still available for terminal users - [ ] Analytics tracks Learning Hub clicks - [ ] Visual hierarchy emphasizes learning journey - [ ] Messaging is clear about the two paths (Learn Hub vs. Advanced) ## Notes - Don't remove the `onboard` command option - some users prefer CLI - Keep the celebration/confetti - it's a nice touch - Consider adding a progress indicator preview showing 0/9 lessons","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:23:18.475445-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:29:33.607249-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:29:33.607249-05:00","close_reason":"Added trackConversion analytics for Learning Hub clicks on Step 12. Removed unused CommandCard import.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-b9v","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bri","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:11.18954-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bags","title":"FEATURE: acfs info Quick Reference Command","description":"# FEATURE: `acfs info` Quick Reference Command ## Purpose Provide a lightning-fast command that shows users their complete setup at a glance—without running any verification checks. ## Relationship to Existing Tools ### `acfs info` vs `acfs doctor` - They Are DIFFERENT | Aspect | `acfs info` | `acfs doctor` | |--------|-------------|---------------| | Purpose | "What do I have?" | "Is it working?" | | Speed | <1 second | 5-30 seconds | | Method | Read state files | Execute verification commands | | Output | Display/reference | Pass/fail diagnostics | | Use case | Quick reference, orientation | Troubleshooting, validation | Think of it like: - `acfs info` = Reading your car's dashboard - `acfs doctor` = Running diagnostic tests on the engine ## Proposed Output Format ``` ╭─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮ │ ACFS Environment Info │ ╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯ System Hostname: vps-12345 IP Address: 203.0.113.42 Uptime: 3 days, 4 hours Ubuntu: 25.10 (Plucky Puffin) Installed Tools (47/52) ✓ Shell: zsh + oh-my-zsh + powerlevel10k ✓ Languages: bun 1.2.x, uv 0.5.x, rust 1.84.x, go 1.23.x ✓ Agents: claude, codex, gemini ✓ Stack: ntm, bv, cass, cm, caam, slb, ubs, am ○ Skipped: docker, lazydocker, postgresql Quick Commands cc Launch Claude Code cod Launch Codex CLI ntm new X Create tmux session lazygit Visual git interface rg "term" Search code Onboard Progress ████████░░ 7/9 lessons (78%) Next: 07_flywheel_loop.md Run 'acfs doctor' for health verification Run 'acfs info --help' for more options ``` ## Output Formats - Terminal (default): Colorful, human-readable - `--json`: Machine-readable for scripting - `--html`: Self-contained HTML page for dashboard - `--minimal`: Just the essentials (IP, key commands) ## Data Sources (Read-Only, No Execution) - `/etc/hostname`, `hostname -I` (cached) - `~/.acfs/state.json` (installation state) - `~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json` (lesson progress) - `uptime` command (single fast call) - Manifest tool list (from manifest or state) ## Implementation Location - Add to: `scripts/lib/info.sh` (new file) - Register in: `acfs.zshrc` acfs() function - Subcommands: `acfs info`, `acfs info --json`, `acfs info --html` ## Non-Goals - NO verification (that's doctor's job) - NO network calls - NO long-running commands - NO password prompts ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Runs in <1 second - [ ] Shows system info (hostname, IP, uptime, OS version) - [ ] Shows installed tools summary (from state, not verification) - [ ] Shows 5-8 most useful quick commands - [ ] Shows onboard progress with visual bar - [ ] --json flag produces valid JSON - [ ] --html flag produces self-contained HTML - [ ] --minimal flag shows condensed output - [ ] Works offline (no network required) - [ ] Integrates cleanly with existing acfs CLI ## Design Considerations ### Why Read State Instead of Verify? Speed. Doctor already handles verification beautifully. Users need a different tool for "remind me what I have" vs "check if it works." Reading state.json takes milliseconds; running 60 verification commands takes seconds to minutes. ### Why Include Quick Commands? The #1 question after setup is "what commands can I run?" Showing the top aliases directly in info output provides immediate value without users needing to find the cheatsheet. ### Why HTML Output? Enables the static dashboard feature without running a web server. User runs `acfs info --html > ~/.acfs/dashboard/index.html` once, then can serve it when needed or open directly in browser.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:52:05.974108-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.630839-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T15:07:45.630839-05:00","close_reason":"Complete implementation: info.sh with terminal, JSON, HTML, minimal output formats; integrated into acfs CLI","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bags","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9y9x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T14:52:18.499096-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bcin","title":"TASK: Add 'Getting Back In' reconnection section to launch-onboarding","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-trkz (Post-Installation Onboarding Experience) ## Summary Add a prominent "Getting Back In" section to launch-onboarding that explains how to reconnect to the VPS after closing the terminal, and how to set up SSH config for easier access. ## Rationale The UX audit identified reconnection as a critical gap: > "If users close their laptop, they don't know how to get back to their work" Users who close their terminal and can't get back in will: - Feel frustrated and stuck - Potentially abandon the project - Flood support with "how do I get back in?" questions ## Content Specification ### Section: "Getting Back In" Place this AFTER "Your First 5 Minutes" but BEFORE existing quick reference cards. \`\`\`tsx

Getting Back In

Closed your terminal? Here's how to reconnect:

{/* Step 1: Open terminal */}

1. Open your terminal app

Ghostty, WezTerm, or Windows Terminal

{/* Step 2: SSH command */}

2. Connect to your VPS

{/* Step 3: Resume session */}

3. Resume your session (if using NTM)

This brings back exactly where you left off - including any running Claude sessions!

{/* SSH Config tip */}
💡 Pro tip: Set up SSH config for easier access

Add this to your local ~/.ssh/config file: 

{
\`Host myserver
    HostName \${vpsIP}
    User ubuntu
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519\`
}

Then just type: ssh myserver

\`\`\` ### Visual Design - Blue-tinted card (different from other sections) - Terminal icon for visual recognition - Numbered steps - SSH config in collapsible details ### Important Notes - Use the user's actual IP from localStorage - Show both Mac and Windows commands where they differ - Keep it above the fold if possible (it's important!) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] "Getting Back In" section exists and is prominent - [ ] Three clear steps: open terminal, SSH, resume session - [ ] SSH command includes user's IP - [ ] NTM commands explained - [ ] SSH config tip in collapsible section - [ ] Works for both Mac and Windows - [ ] Mobile-friendly ## Dependencies - TASK: Add 'Your First 5 Minutes' section (comes before this in the page) ## Files Modified - apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:55.828607-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:46:09.958363-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:46:09.958363-05:00","close_reason":"Getting Back In section added with 3 steps (open terminal, SSH connect with user's IP, resume NTM), collapsible SSH config tip, Mac/Windows commands. All quality gates pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-bcin","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:22:22.834242-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

@@ -90,7 +90,9 @@ NO_DEPS=false
 # Resume/reinstall options (used by state.sh confirm_resume)
 export ACFS_FORCE_RESUME=false
 export ACFS_FORCE_REINSTALL=false
-export ACFS_INTERACTIVE=false
+# NOTE: When unset/empty, downstream libs default to interactive behavior when a TTY is available.
+# install.sh forces non-interactive behavior in --yes mode.
+export ACFS_INTERACTIVE="${ACFS_INTERACTIVE:-}"
 RESET_STATE_ONLY=false
 
 # Preflight options
@@ -125,133 +127,17 @@ fi
 export _ACFS_LOGGING_SH_LOADED=1
 
 # ============================================================
-# Source context tracking library for try_step() wrapper
+# Minimal error-tracking fallbacks
+# These are replaced once scripts/lib/error_tracking.sh is sourced (detect_environment()).
 # ============================================================
-_source_context_lib() {
-    # Already loaded?
-    if [[ -n "${ACFS_CONTEXT_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
-        return 0
-    fi
-
-    # Try local file first (when running from repo)
-    if [[ -n "${SCRIPT_DIR:-}" ]] && [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/scripts/lib/context.sh" ]]; then
-        # shellcheck source=scripts/lib/context.sh
-        source "$SCRIPT_DIR/scripts/lib/context.sh"
-        return 0
-    fi
-
-    # Try relative path (when running from repo root)
-    if [[ -f "./scripts/lib/context.sh" ]]; then
-        source "./scripts/lib/context.sh"
-        return 0
-    fi
-
-    # Download for curl|bash scenario (if curl available)
-    if command -v curl &>/dev/null; then
-        local tmp_context=""
-        if command -v mktemp &>/dev/null; then
-            tmp_context="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/acfs-context.XXXXXX" 2>/dev/null)" || tmp_context=""
-        fi
-        if [[ -n "$tmp_context" ]] && curl "${ACFS_EARLY_CURL_ARGS[@]}" "$ACFS_RAW/scripts/lib/context.sh" -o "$tmp_context" 2>/dev/null; then
-            source "$tmp_context"
-            rm -f "$tmp_context"
-            return 0
-        fi
-    fi
-
-    # Fallback: define minimal no-op stubs so install.sh still works
-    set_phase() { :; }
-    try_step() { shift; "$@"; }
-    try_step_eval() { shift; bash -e -o pipefail -c "$1"; }
-    return 0
-}
-_source_context_lib
+type -t set_phase &>/dev/null || set_phase() { :; }
+type -t try_step &>/dev/null || try_step() { shift; "$@"; }
+type -t try_step_eval &>/dev/null || try_step_eval() { shift; bash -e -o pipefail -c "$1"; }
 
 # ============================================================
-# Source reliability libraries for state tracking & reporting
-# (mjt.5.8: Integrate manifest-driven execution with resume/state)
+# Installer libraries are sourced later in main() via detect_environment(), after
+# bootstrapping the repo archive for curl|bash runs (prevents mixed refs).
 # ============================================================
-_source_reliability_libs() {
-    # Already loaded?
-    if [[ -n "${ACFS_RELIABILITY_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
-        return 0
-    fi
-
-    local loaded_state=false
-    local loaded_report=false
-
-    # Try local files first (when running from repo)
-    if [[ -n "${SCRIPT_DIR:-}" ]]; then
-        local state_lib="$SCRIPT_DIR/scripts/lib/state.sh"
-        local report_lib="$SCRIPT_DIR/scripts/lib/report.sh"
-
-        if [[ -f "$state_lib" ]]; then
-            # shellcheck source=scripts/lib/state.sh
-            source "$state_lib" && loaded_state=true
-        fi
-
-        if [[ -f "$report_lib" ]]; then
-            # shellcheck source=scripts/lib/report.sh
-            source "$report_lib" && loaded_report=true
-        fi
-    fi
-
-    # If local files weren't loaded, try downloading (curl|bash scenario)
-    if [[ "$loaded_state" != "true" || "$loaded_report" != "true" ]]; then
-        if command -v curl &>/dev/null; then
-            local tmp_state=""
-            local tmp_report=""
-
-            if [[ "$loaded_state" != "true" ]]; then
-                if command -v mktemp &>/dev/null; then
-                    tmp_state="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/acfs-state.XXXXXX" 2>/dev/null)" || tmp_state=""
-                fi
-                if [[ -n "$tmp_state" ]] && curl "${ACFS_EARLY_CURL_ARGS[@]}" "$ACFS_RAW/scripts/lib/state.sh" -o "$tmp_state" 2>/dev/null; then
-                    source "$tmp_state" && loaded_state=true
-                    rm -f "$tmp_state"
-                fi
-            fi
-
-            if [[ "$loaded_report" != "true" ]]; then
-                if command -v mktemp &>/dev/null; then
-                    tmp_report="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/acfs-report.XXXXXX" 2>/dev/null)" || tmp_report=""
-                fi
-                if [[ -n "$tmp_report" ]] && curl "${ACFS_EARLY_CURL_ARGS[@]}" "$ACFS_RAW/scripts/lib/report.sh" -o "$tmp_report" 2>/dev/null; then
-                    source "$tmp_report" && loaded_report=true
-                    rm -f "$tmp_report"
-                fi
-            fi
-        fi
-    fi
-
-    # Define fallback stubs for any functions that weren't loaded
-    # This ensures the installer works even if libs fail to load
-    if ! type -t state_init &>/dev/null; then
-        state_init() { :; }
-    fi
-    if ! type -t state_phase_start &>/dev/null; then
-        state_phase_start() { :; }
-    fi
-    if ! type -t state_phase_complete &>/dev/null; then
-        state_phase_complete() { :; }
-    fi
-    if ! type -t state_phase_fail &>/dev/null; then
-        state_phase_fail() { :; }
-    fi
-    if ! type -t confirm_resume &>/dev/null; then
-        confirm_resume() { return 1; }  # Fresh install
-    fi
-    if ! type -t report_failure &>/dev/null; then
-        report_failure() { echo "Installation failed" >&2; }
-    fi
-    if ! type -t report_success &>/dev/null; then
-        report_success() { echo "Installation complete" >&2; }
-    fi
-
-    export ACFS_RELIABILITY_LOADED=1
-    return 0

-_source_reliability_libs
 
 # ============================================================
 # Source Ubuntu upgrade library for auto-upgrade functionality (nb4)
@@ -262,6 +148,14 @@ _source_ubuntu_upgrade_lib() {
         return 0
     fi
 
+    # Prefer bootstrapped libs when available (curl|bash mode), to avoid mixed refs.
+    if [[ -n "${ACFS_LIB_DIR:-}" ]] && [[ -f "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/ubuntu_upgrade.sh" ]]; then
+        # shellcheck source=scripts/lib/ubuntu_upgrade.sh
+        source "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/ubuntu_upgrade.sh"
+        export ACFS_UBUNTU_UPGRADE_LOADED=1
+        return 0
+    fi
+
     # Try local file first (when running from repo)
     if [[ -n "${SCRIPT_DIR:-}" ]] && [[ -f "$SCRIPT_DIR/scripts/lib/ubuntu_upgrade.sh" ]]; then
         # shellcheck source=scripts/lib/ubuntu_upgrade.sh
@@ -836,12 +730,31 @@ detect_environment() {
         source "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/state.sh"
     fi
 
+    # Source error pattern matcher (report.sh uses get_suggested_fix when available).
+    if [[ -f "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/errors.sh" ]]; then
+        # shellcheck source=scripts/lib/errors.sh
+        source "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/errors.sh"
+    fi
+
+    # Source structured failure/success reporting (mjt.5.8).
+    if [[ -f "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/report.sh" ]]; then
+        # shellcheck source=scripts/lib/report.sh
+        source "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/report.sh"
+    fi
+
     # Source error tracking for try_step wrappers (mjt.5.8)
     if [[ -f "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/error_tracking.sh" ]]; then
         # shellcheck source=scripts/lib/error_tracking.sh
         source "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/error_tracking.sh"
     fi
 
+    # Source Ubuntu upgrade library from the same lib dir when available (nb4).
+    if [[ -f "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/ubuntu_upgrade.sh" ]]; then
+        # shellcheck source=scripts/lib/ubuntu_upgrade.sh
+        source "$ACFS_LIB_DIR/ubuntu_upgrade.sh"
+        export ACFS_UBUNTU_UPGRADE_LOADED=1
+    fi

+# Execute a command string with try_step semantics (for pipelines/compound commands)
+# Usage: try_step_eval "description" "command string"
+try_step_eval() {
+    local description="$1"
+    local command_str="${2:-}"
+
+    if [[ -z "$command_str" ]]; then
+        if type -t log_error &>/dev/null; then
+            log_error "try_step_eval: missing command string for: $description"
+        fi
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    try_step "$description" bash -e -o pipefail -c "$command_str"
+}
+
